notes: Library card sections completed for parent choice: reading, vocabulary, journals, writing, questions, informational text, and assessments.
Structured from existing markdown unit card.

book.title: The Secret Garden
book.author: Frances Hodgson Burnett
book.description: Author: Frances Hodgson Burnett Grade Level: 4th Grade (Ages 9-10) Unit Length: 30 lessons (24 regular + 6 assessments) Days: 151-180 (Weeks 31-36) Theme: Healing, Growth, Transformation, Nature's Power Reading Level: Classic novel This card now includes complete weekly library sections for Grade 4 use.

weeks.title: Week 31: Mary Arrives (Days 151-155)
reading: There Is No One Left, Mistress Mary, Across the Moor, Martha
journal: What does this week's reading show about Healing? Use one scene as evidence.
Choose one character decision from There Is No One Left, Mistress Mary, Across the Moor, Martha. Would you make the same choice? Explain why.
writing: Write an ACE paragraph answering one comprehension question with text evidence.
Draft a short narrative response connected to this week's reading.
questions: What problem or conflict matters most in There Is No One Left, Mistress Mary, Across the Moor, Martha?
Which detail best reveals a character trait, motivation, or change?
How does the setting affect what happens in this section?
What lesson, theme, or big idea is starting to develop?
informationalText: Gardens, soil, and seasonal plant growth
assessment: Weekly assessment or review day included in source card.
